The Problem
Germany's Care Crisis — the Numbers
Scale of the crisis
Germany needs more nurses, trains fewer, and loses 1 in 4 along the way.

Three interconnected failures create a system under critical strain. Lingocare is designed to address all three simultaneously.

The Gap
~500K
Nurses missing by 2030
•115K–200K already missing today
•Annual demand (~75K) outpaces graduates (~50K)
•Up to 690K projected shortfall by 2049
The Pipeline
~1,500
Pflegeschulen nationwide
•~57,000 begin training each year
•Mostly large hospital-affiliated schools
•Hundreds of small schools lack digital infrastructure
The Leak
1 in 4
Drop out mid-training
•Language barrier — especially international trainees
•No personalised support during placement
•Generic, non-adaptive learning materials
Root Causes
Why learners drop out
Arrival — Language mismatch
International trainees arrive with A2–B1 German. Patient communication requires B2+. No tool teaches care German in context.
Month 3–6 — Generic content fails them
Materials aren't adapted to level, mother tongue, or learning style. Same content for a German-native and someone 6 months in the country.
Clinical placement — No structured support
Learners face real-world clinical German with zero support. Teachers can't monitor. Directors are blind to progress here.
Dropout — Avoidable with early intervention
By the time a director notices a risk, the learner has already mentally left. Without real-time data, intervention is always too late.
Why schools struggle
📄
Content from scratch
Teachers spend 60%+ of prep time creating materials. No AI, no reuse, no curriculum alignment.
👁️‍🗨️
No visibility
Directors discover delayed modules weeks after the fact. No real-time dashboard to catch problems early.
🔌
No digital infra
Small Pflegeschulen can't afford enterprise EdTech. University tools don't fit nursing's structure.
🌍
International cohorts
30–40% of trainees in many schools are international. Their needs are fundamentally different.
💡
The opportunity is clear.
A platform that closes language gaps, adapts to every learner, and gives schools real-time visibility. Until Lingocare, it didn't exist.
The Solution
One intelligent system. Three connected challenges.
🗣️
Language + Care
Clinical German, in context
All content built on Lingocare's pedagogical framework. One system that teaches German through care, and care in German. Levels A2 → B2+ Advanced Clinical.
A2 → B2+ Advanced Clinical
🎯
Personalisation
AI that adapts to each learner
Adapts to mother tongue, learning style, current level, and clinical specialisation. No two journeys are the same.
Mother tongue aware
Level-adaptive
🏫
Built for the whole school
Director → Teacher → Learner
Proprietary pedagogical framework co-developed with nursing educators. Real-time visibility for directors. AI tools for teachers. Starting with Hessen's curriculum.
Hessen curriculum mapped

Two-phased approach
01
Phase 1 — Live Now
Active in classrooms
API-orchestrated system deployed in real Pflegeschulen. 5 pilot cohorts completed with student-fit validation. Hessen curriculum fully mapped. Teacher tools and learner app in daily use.
02
Phase 2 — In Development
Building now
Proprietary Lingocare LLM fine-tuned on pedagogical framework + clinical German corpus. Domain-specific nursing-language intelligence. Every cohort improves the next version.

Our edge
Class-first AI. Every cohort improves the next version.
Every feature is tested in a real classroom before it ships. Every learner interaction trains the next version of the model. The more schools use Lingocare, the better it gets — for every school.

Platform in Action · Real Screens
PF 2 — Analysieren · Instructor Analytics
● Live PlatformClass 9B · 15 Students

Real screens from the Lingocare platform — taken from an active exercise session (PF 2 — Analysieren) with a pilot class. This is what instructors use every day.

Klassenübersicht
Antwortverteilung
Paarweise Antworten
Task Overview — what the instructor sees
Bar chart of every student: Decision Score vs Activation Score. Submission status panel on the right. "Schwache Leistung" auto-flags at-risk students (Yesha Test 0/7, Student 15 0/60).
9
Eingereicht
von 15
7
In Bearbeitung
6
Nicht begonnen
⚠️ Schwache Leistung: 3 students
Studentenübersicht table below chart
What this tells the instructor
📊
Decision Score vs Activation Score
AI scores each student on decision quality and engagement. Both shown per student.
📋
Full table: Punktzahl + Schwache/Starke Bereiche
Every student row clickable. Weak areas and strengths per task visible at a glance.
Answer Distribution — task by task
For every sub-task: which answers students chose and whether they were right or wrong. Red bars = wrong, green = correct. AI flags "Confusion between student answers detected" and recommends "Need Class Discussion".
Task 1: Verorten · 0%
Task 1: Verorten · 17%
Task 1: Verorten · 60%
Task 2: Kommunikation · 0%
AI-powered class-level insight
🧠
"Need Class Discussion" flag
When confusion is detected across students, AI proactively flags for class discussion. A recommendation, not just a score.
📐
Per-task, per-answer breakdown
Aufgabe 1A, 1B, 1C, Aufgabe 2 shown separately. Instructor knows exactly where the class went wrong.
Paired Peer Responses — collaborative learning tracked
Students paired in groups (Group Z, Group Y). Platform shows how each pair categorised answers — Personenbezogen, Organisationsbezogen, Situationsbezogen. Compare reasoning across pairs in real time.
Personenbezogen
Organisationsbezogen
Situationsbezogen
Collaborative learning, tracked
👥
Group-by-group pair view
Both students' categorisations visible side-by-side. Where they agree vs. disagree is immediate.
🔍
Gemeinsame Begründung shown
Each group's shared rationale visible. Search by student or answer across all groups.
This is Phase 1. Live. In real classrooms.
5 pilot cohorts validated. These screens come from real student data. Every session trains the next version.

KGK – 4.4 · Card 1 of 4
Chain of Infection: Basics
🔗 Das Modell der Kette
PATHOGENS can migrate from one person to another. The path of the germs is called the INFECTION CHAIN.

In care, we have to stop this chain as early as possible. This is how we protect the residents and ourselves.

All HYGIENE MEASURES help to break the chain.
🦠 Source
→
📡 Transmission
→
👤 New Host
🔗
Key Terms
Pathogen — disease-causing microorganism
Infection chain — path from source to host
Hygiene measures — actions that break the chain
Test Your Knowledge
Breaking the INFECTION CHAIN is the most important task in INFECTION PREVENTION. True or False?
✓ Correct! Breaking any link in the infection chain prevents spread.
Card 1 / 4 · KGK-4.4
🎉
+150 XP Earned!

Content Studio · AI-Powered
Build smarter content, in minutes.

Lingocare's Content Studio gives teachers AI superpowers — guided by our proprietary pedagogical framework. From topic to published content in under 5 minutes.

The content problem we solve
Today: 3–4 hours per topic
Research, write, format, create questions. Still not adapted to individual learner levels.
With Lingocare Studio: under 5 minutes
Pick a topic. Hit Magic Write. Review and refine. Publish. Content is automatically Hessen-aligned.
Phase 2 — Self-improving content
The Lingocare LLM learns from learner interactions. Content improves based on what worked in your classroom.
Three-step structure
1
Knowledge Anchor (KA)
The core teaching card. Text + image + key terms + test question. The building block of all Lingocare content.
2
Exercise — 12 Building Blocks
Drag to Sort, Tap to Mark, Multiple Choice, Free Text, Scale Selector, Branching Simulation, Word Chip Assembly, and more.
3
Publish & Track
One-click publish to assigned classes. Automatic tracking of completion, time-on-task, and quiz scores.

Which actions break the infection chain?
Regular hand hygiene after patient contact
Wearing appropriate PPE (gloves, mask)
Proper disposal of contaminated material
Sharing instruments between patients
